Components are stacked vertically in the natural journey order: therapist summary → bio and fees → appointment type → scheduling configuration → availability grid → selected slot summary/actions. Each section is clearly separated with generous vertical spacing, and there are no multi-column splits; any internal grouping (e.g., within the availability grid) still respects a primary vertical reading flow.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: All therapist profile elements sit below the existing site header and hero band inside a single, centered content column. Components are stacked vertically in the natural journey order: therapist summary → bio and fees → appointment type → scheduling configuration → availability grid → selected slot summary/actions. Each section is clearly separated with generous vertical spacing, and there are no multi-column splits; any internal grouping (e.g., within the availability grid) still respects a primary vertical reading flow.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: The main content lives in a centered container roughly 60–70% of the viewport width (max-width ~1080–1200px), with soft off-white/light green backgrounds. The therapist summary and bio occupy the upper third of the main content area, forming the informational context. The scheduling controls and availability grid occupy the middle third, with more vertical depth for the grid. The selected-slot action bar is given full column width near the bottom (or as a subtle sticky element), but with low visual weight. Section spacing uses 32–48px margins between major blocks and 16–24px padding inside cards and panels, following the 8px base spacing scale.
layout_strategies.content_density: Text and controls are kept low-density with ample breathing room: headings have clear separation from body text, form controls are not tightly stacked, and the availability grid uses comfortable line height and cell spacing. Bullets and lists have 8–12px of vertical space between items. The overall feel is calm and unhurried, avoiding dense tables or tightly packed controls.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: The eye moves from the global header and hero (with the page or therapist title centered) down into the left-aligned therapist summary at the top of the column. From there, it flows vertically through the bio and fees, then into the appointment type selector and scheduling configuration. The availability grid becomes the main interactive focal point, drawing attention to available time slots. After a slot is selected, attention naturally drops to the selected-slot summary and primary action button. This keeps a gentle Z-then-vertical pattern: header navigation → hero title center → therapist summary left → straight down through the scheduling elements.

layout.overall_description: Below the site header and the full-width hero band (showing the therapist profile title or therapist’s name over a soft leaf image), the page transitions into a light, slightly green-tinted content area. A centered column, about two-thirds of the viewport width, anchors all therapist content. At the top of this column, a serene therapist summary section introduces the provider with name, credentials, and key specializations, breathing in generous space above and below. Just beneath, a softly padded panel presents the therapist’s bio and fee information as readable paragraphs and simple lists. Further down, the page gradually shifts from informational to interactive: first an appointment type selector, then a scheduling configuration panel that feels like part of the same flow. Below these controls, a bordered, lightly elevated availability area shows days and time slots in a clean grid, spanning the width of the column but never feeling crowded. A subtle ‘see more times’ link sits just below, aligned with the left edge of the text. Once a time is chosen, a calm, understated action bar near the bottom of the viewport summarizes the selection and offers a primary button to proceed, mirroring the gentle, centered rhythm of the rest of the page. Throughout, ample vertical spacing and consistent alignment create a relaxed, reassuring experience that feels coherent with the minimalist, nature-inspired aesthetic of the original mockup.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The therapist-profile-loader occupies the entire main content column directly below the hero band while profile data is being fetched. It spans roughly 60–70% of the viewport width, centered horizontally with equal left and right margins, and extends vertically to cover the space that will later be filled by the therapist summary, bio, scheduling controls, and availability grid. Skeleton elements mimic the future layout: a wide rectangular bar near the top representing the summary header, a larger block below resembling the bio and fees section, followed by a few horizontal strips suggesting form fields and a grid placeholder for availability. There is 24–32px of vertical spacing between each skeleton block, maintaining the airy feel even in the loading state.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Temporarily replaces the entire therapist content column while loading; flows with the document and disappears once data is loaded, handing space over to the actual components.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The therapist-summary-header sits at the top of the main content column, starting approximately 48px below the bottom edge of the hero band to avoid crowding. It spans the full width of the centered column (about 60–70% of the viewport, up to 1080–1200px) with 24px of internal padding on all sides. Content is left-aligned within this block, with the therapist’s name in a slightly larger, serif-like heading that echoes the elegance of the hero’s typography. A small portrait or icon (if present) aligns to the left, with name, credentials, and key tags/details stacked to its right or directly below in a single column on narrower screens. The section has a subtle off-white or very pale green background and, optionally, a soft shadow or faint border to distinguish it from the page background. There is a 32–40px gap below this header before the bio and fees section begins.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Flows with the document as the first visible section of therapist content; no sticky or fixed behavior.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The therapist-bio-and-fees component is positioned immediately below the therapist-summary-header, separated by roughly 32px of vertical space. It also spans the full width of the central column and uses a card-like container with 24px padding on all sides, mirroring the summary’s visual treatment for consistency. Inside, a left-aligned subheading (e.g., “About” or “Bio”) sits at the top with a comfortable 16–24px gap before the body text. Paragraphs of bio copy are arranged in a single column with generous line height (around 1.5–1.7) and 12–16px of space between paragraphs. Fee information appears below the bio as a short list or bullet points, each list item separated by 8–12px of vertical space to maintain low density. The entire section has a light, calm background (pale green-tinted or off-white) that blends smoothly with the page, and a 40–48px gap below it leads into the interactive appointment type selector.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Standard block-level section within the column; no special positioning.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The appointment-type-selector appears directly below the bio and fees section, with approximately 40px of vertical separation. It remains within the same central column width and may be rendered as a simple, low-profile horizontal or vertical list of options (e.g., radio buttons or pill-shaped buttons) grouped inside a subtle panel with 16–24px padding. A small, left-aligned label or heading (e.g., “Select Appointment Type”) sits at the top of this panel, with 12–16px of spacing before the options. If options are laid out horizontally on larger screens, they should still feel loosely spaced with at least 16px of horizontal gap between them and no more than two rows, reverting to a simple vertical stack on narrower devices. There is about 24–32px of space below this selector before the scheduling configuration panel begins, keeping the flow clearly segmented but visually connected.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Inline, flowing with the rest of the content; adapts from horizontal option grouping on wider screens to vertical stacking on smaller screens while staying within the central column.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The scheduling-configuration-panel sits beneath the appointment-type-selector, separated by roughly 24–32px of vertical space, and spans the full width of the central column. It is a form-like block with 16–24px of internal padding, divided vertically into labeled controls such as date selectors, in-person vs telehealth toggles, and weekend availability switches. Each field is presented in a single column to preserve the calm, linear reading pattern: label on top, control directly underneath, with 16px of vertical spacing between stacked fields. On larger screens, closely related controls (e.g., date and time range) may appear side-by-side but still within the same visual panel, with at least 16–24px horizontal spacing between them and enough padding to avoid crowding. A small 16–24px gap separates the bottom of this panel from the availability-loader/availability-grid region below, visually linking them as one workflow step while keeping the page uncluttered.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Flows with the document; form fields expand or wrap vertically on narrow screens to maintain a simple, readable column layout.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The availability-loader occupies the exact region where the availability-grid will appear, directly beneath the scheduling-configuration-panel and above the see-more-times-link. It spans the full width of the central column and has a height roughly equivalent to several rows of appointment slots, giving the sense of an upcoming grid. Skeleton lines or faint blocks represent date headers and time rows, aligned in a loose grid pattern but with 12–16px gaps between rows to retain the design’s spacious feel. There is a 24–32px margin above and below this loading block so that even in loading state, the area sits comfortably within the page’s rhythm.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Appears only while availability data is loading, replacing the availability-grid in place; it flows inline with content and disappears once slots are loaded.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The availability-grid sits centrally below the scheduling-configuration-panel, taking up the full width of the content column (roughly 60–70% of the viewport) and forming the main interactive focus area for choosing a time. It appears as a softly bordered or subtly elevated panel with 16–24px of padding. At the top of the panel, a quiet, left-aligned heading or label (e.g., “Available Times”) is followed by date labels arranged horizontally (for a week view) or vertically, depending on the interaction model, with at least 16px of spacing between them. Below these, time slots are rendered as discrete, pill-like buttons or lightly outlined boxes arranged in a simple grid: typically 2–4 columns of slots per row on larger screens, wrapping into more rows on smaller screens. Each slot has ample internal padding (8–12px vertically, 16–20px horizontally) and at least 8–12px of spacing from adjacent slots, so the grid feels open rather than cramped. Empty days or unavailable sections are represented with calm, low-contrast text or gentle placeholders rather than heavy, dark blocks. The bottom of the grid panel sits about 16–24px above the see-more-times-link, leaving just enough space to clearly separate the clickable times from the navigation link.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Inline within the document flow; internally responsive grid that reduces the number of columns and increases vertical stacking on smaller screens while remaining within the central column.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The selected-slot-summary-and-actions component appears below the availability-grid and see-more-times-link but behaves like a light, focused action bar. On initial load (before a slot is selected), it can either be hidden or presented as a faint, low-height placeholder. Once a time is selected, it expands into a clearly defined horizontal bar that spans the full width of the main content column. Inside, the left side (or top on smaller screens) shows a concise summary of the chosen therapist, appointment type, date, and time in a single line or two short lines, with text left-aligned and comfortably spaced using 8–12px between summary elements. On the right side (or below on narrow devices), a primary action button (“Continue”/“Review Appointment”) is right-aligned with 16–24px of horizontal padding and enough space around it to avoid visual tension. There is 24–32px of margin above this bar to separate it from the grid area. The background is a slightly more prominent off-white or pale green, differentiating it gently from the rest of the page without feeling urgent. The overall height remains modest so it feels like a supportive footer for the flow rather than a dominant banner.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Initially flows as a normal block following the availability section; once a slot is selected and the user scrolls such that the bar would leave the viewport, it can adopt a subtle sticky behavior—sticking to the bottom edge of the viewport within the central column—especially on desktop, while still respecting mobile constraints.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The draft-appointment-creator works behind the scenes in close association with the selected-slot-summary-and-actions and availability-grid components. Visually, it does not introduce a separate panel; its presence is represented by subtle state changes in existing elements. For example, when the user clicks the primary action in the selected-slot bar, the button may briefly show a soft loading state (spinner or text change) while the draft is created, and the summary within the bar remains in place, reaffirming the selection. Any feedback it produces (e.g., errors) is surfaced via the error-and-empty-state-handler in the relevant section rather than through its own block.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Non-visual, inline behavioral component tied logically to the selected-slot-summary-and-actions; it does not occupy dedicated layout space beyond minor loading indicators within existing elements.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The see-more-times-link is positioned directly beneath the availability-grid panel, with a 16–24px vertical gap to separate it visually from the grid while still signaling that it controls the same content region. It is left-aligned with the text inside the availability-grid, starting at the same horizontal baseline as the grid’s left padding, which reinforces the single-column alignment. The link text is simple and unobtrusive, using a calm accent color (a muted green or complementary tone) and perhaps a subtle underline on hover, but not a large button treatment. There is about 24–32px of space between this link and the selected-slot-summary-and-actions bar below, preserving the airy rhythm even around navigational elements.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Inline navigation element; remains in place as the user scrolls, potentially triggering changes in the availability-grid (e.g., loading more days below or paging horizontally) without changing its own position.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The error-and-empty-state-handler manifests as gentle, inline message panels that appear in place of or directly above the components they relate to. For example, if there is an error loading therapist data, a softly bordered message card appears centered in the main content column where the therapist-summary-header and bio would normally be, with 24px of padding and 24–32px of vertical margin from surrounding elements. The message is left-aligned, with a clear, concise heading (e.g., “We’re having trouble loading this therapist right now”) and a short explanatory line, plus a small, calm call-to-action if needed (e.g., “Try again”). For availability-specific errors or empty states, the message card appears inside the availability-grid region, replacing the grid but respecting its width and padding. Empty states (e.g., no slots available) use soft icons or simple text and preserve the generous spacing, avoiding dense or alarming visuals. All such messages inherit the central column width and align with the same left margin as other text, maintaining a continuous visual rhythm.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Contextual and inline; each message occupies the same space as the component it replaces (summary area, availability area, etc.). It does not float or overlay the entire page, ensuring the overall layout structure remains stable even when states change.

name: Book Appointment
title: Book Appointment
description: Guided booking page to choose an appointment type, refine search filters, view therapist availability, and create a draft appointment for confirmation.

core_features: Load available appointment types and require the user to select one before searching for therapists, using getAppointmentTypeOptionsForBooking.
Expose booking filters (specialty, format, ZIP code, date range, time of day, weekends-only) populated where relevant by getTherapistDirectoryFilterOptions and synchronized with URL parameters.

Search therapists who have at least one matching appointment slot for the selected appointment type and filters, using searchTherapistsForAppointment with sortBy set to prioritize next available appointments.
Display therapist search results, including each therapist’s first matching appointment slot (if any), and provide a “View availability” action for each therapist.
Show a therapist-specific availability grid for the selected appointment type and filter constraints by calling getTherapistAvailabilityForAppointment.
Allow the user to select a specific appointment slot from the availability grid and create a draft appointment via createDraftAppointmentFromSlot.

Navigate the user to appointment_confirm.html with the draft appointment ID as a URL parameter after successful slot selection, or to therapists.html with current filters when they choose to view the full directory.
Maintain booking state (appointment type and filter criteria) in URL parameters so the page can be reloaded or shared while preserving the current search context.
user_workflows:
Workflow 1: Start a new booking
1. User opens book_appointment.html, optionally with URL parameters for appointmentTypeCode, specialty, format, zip, dateStart, dateEnd, timeOfDay, and weekendsOnly.
2. Page initialization controller reads URL parameters.
3. The page calls getAppointmentTypeOptionsForBooking to retrieve all bookable appointment types.
4. The appointment-type selector highlights the appointmentTypeCode from the URL if provided; otherwise, no type is selected and therapist search is disabled until the user chooses a type.
5. The page calls getTherapistDirectoryFilterOptions to populate specialty and format filter options; initial filter values are set based on URL parameters (specialty, format, zip, dateStart, dateEnd, timeOfDay, weekendsOnly).
6. When an appointment type is selected (or already provided in the URL), the booking-page controller uses the current appointmentTypeCode and filter values to call searchTherapistsForAppointment with sortBy set to prioritize next available appointments, and with limit/offset for pagination.
7. The therapist-results-list component renders the returned therapists, including each therapist’s firstMatchingSlot if available, and shows a “View availability” action for each result.
8. The user refines filters (e.g., changes date range, toggles time-of-day or weekends-only constraints, or adjusts specialty/format/ZIP); each change updates the URL parameters and triggers a new call to searchTherapistsForAppointment.
9. The user clicks “View availability” on a therapist result to inspect more options for that therapist, which opens the availability grid for that therapist.

Workflow 2: Select a slot and create a draft appointment
1. After clicking “View availability” for a therapist, the page calls getTherapistAvailabilityForAppointment with the therapistId, the currently selected appointmentTypeCode, and the current constraints (date range, session format, location ZIP, timeOfDay, weekendsOnly) inferred from the filter state and URL.
2. The therapist-availability-panel groups and displays the returned slots as an availability grid.
3. The user examines available slots and selects a preferred slot. The component can assist by visually indicating the earliest slot that satisfies time-of-day or weekend constraints, but the user can choose any available slot from the returned set.
4. When the user confirms their choice (e.g., by clicking a “Book this time” or “Continue” button associated with the selected slot), the slot-booking-controller calls createDraftAppointmentFromSlot with the chosen appointmentSlotId.
5. If createDraftAppointmentFromSlot returns success, the controller reads the returned Appointment.id and redirects the browser to appointment_confirm.html with appointmentId as a URL parameter.
6. If createDraftAppointmentFromSlot returns an error (success is false), the controller stays on the page, surfaces the message to the user, and allows them to pick another slot or try again without losing their current filters.

Workflow 3: Pre-filtered booking from another page and optional switch to full directory
1. A user clicks a “Book” or “Find an appointment” link elsewhere on the site (e.g., index.html) that navigates to book_appointment.html with one or more URL parameters such as appointmentTypeCode, specialty, format, zip, dateStart, dateEnd, timeOfDay, or weekendsOnly.
2. On load, the booking-page-init controller reads these URL parameters, initializes the appointment-type selector, and pre-fills filters in the appointment-filter-bar.
3. As soon as an appointment type is resolved (either from URL or user input), the page calls searchTherapistsForAppointment with filters derived from these parameters and displays matching therapists.
4. If the user wants to explore the full directory rather than booking directly, they click the “View therapists” link.
5. The view-therapists-link component constructs a URL to therapists.html, including the current specialty, format, and zip filter values as URL parameters.
6. The browser navigates to therapists.html, where those parameters are used to initialize the therapist directory filters, providing a consistent context between the booking page and the full directory.
interactions: Selecting or changing the appointment type in the appointment-type selector updates the appointmentTypeCode in the URL, clears any therapist results tied to a previous type, and triggers a new call to searchTherapistsForAppointment once the new type is set.

Changing any filter input (specialty, format, ZIP, date range, time of day, weekends-only) updates the corresponding URL parameter(s) and triggers a debounced or explicit search submission that calls searchTherapistsForAppointment with updated filters and resets pagination offset.

Using quick-select date range options (such as a control that sets the next several days) updates dateStart/dateEnd in the URL and refreshes therapist search results and subsequent availability grids with that date range.

Clicking the sort control in the therapist-results-list sets or changes sortBy (for example, to emphasize next available appointments) and re-calls searchTherapistsForAppointment with the new sortBy value.

Clicking “View availability” for a therapist sets the currently expanded therapist identifier in page state (and optionally a URL parameter) and calls getTherapistAvailabilityForAppointment with the current appointment type and filter constraints.

Selecting an appointment slot in the therapist-availability-panel marks that slot as the currentSelection within the component and enables the booking action button controlled by the slot-booking-controller.

Clicking the booking action button after selecting a slot triggers createDraftAppointmentFromSlot; on success, the user is navigated to appointment_confirm.html with appointmentId in the URL; on failure, an error message is displayed and the selected slot remains highlighted.

Clicking pagination controls changes the offset (or page) parameter in the URL and re-invokes searchTherapistsForAppointment to fetch the next or previous set of therapists while keeping the current appointment type and filters.
Clicking the “View therapists” link navigates to therapists.html with the current specialty, format, and zip filter values passed as URL parameters to preserve context.

Reloading or sharing the page URL restores the previously selected appointment type and filter values from URL parameters, then re-calls getAppointmentTypeOptionsForBooking, getTherapistDirectoryFilterOptions, and searchTherapistsForAppointment as needed to rebuild the state.
page_functionality.state_logic: The canonical state of the Book Appointment page is maintained through URL parameters rather than local storage. On initial load, the page reads the following known parameters: appointmentTypeCode, specialty, format, zip, dateStart, dateEnd, timeOfDay, weekendsOnly, plus optional internal parameters such as sortBy, limit, and offset used for pagination and sorting. These parameters initialize the appointment-type selector and filter form. After initialization, any change in appointment type or filter values updates the corresponding URL parameters via the browser history API (e.g., updating the query string), ensuring the URL always reflects the current state. When appointmentTypeCode is present and valid, the page calls searchTherapistsForAppointment with: (1) appointmentTypeCode taken from the URL; (2) a filters object composed from specialty, format (mapped to one or more session formats), zip (mapped to location ZIP), dateStart/dateEnd, timeOfDay, and weekendsOnly; and (3) sortBy/limit/offset derived from URL or defaults. If appointmentTypeCode is missing or invalid, therapist search is withheld until the user selects a valid type; the page still loads filter options for display. The therapist-availability-panel does not introduce new filter state; instead, it reuses the same URL-derived appointmentTypeCode, dateStart/dateEnd, format, zip, timeOfDay, and weekendsOnly when calling getTherapistAvailabilityForAppointment for a specific therapist. Slot selection is transient state inside the availability panel and is not encoded in the URL; only after the user confirms a selection is createDraftAppointmentFromSlot invoked. On success, navigation to appointment_confirm.html passes only appointmentId as a URL parameter; details like therapistId or slotId are retrieved on that page using its own interfaces. The “View therapists” link constructs a navigation URL to therapists.html from the current specialty, format, and zip URL parameters, without modifying them, so the same filter context is preserved between pages.

components.functionality: On page load, parses URL parameters, initializes appointment type and filter state, triggers loading of appointment types and filter options, and conditionally starts the therapist search when a valid appointment type is present.
Displays all available appointment types for booking, allows the user to select one as the first step of the flow, and updates the booking context and URL based on the selection.

Provides filter controls for specialty, session format, ZIP code, date range, time of day, and weekends-only. Initializes its options using therapist directory filter metadata and keeps its values synchronized with URL parameters. Any change triggers a therapist search refresh when an appointment type is selected.

Renders the list of therapists returned by searchTherapistsForAppointment, including each therapist’s core details and their firstMatchingSlot. Supports sorting (e.g., next available) and pagination, and exposes actions such as viewing detailed availability for each therapist.

When a user selects 'View availability' for a therapist, this component loads the therapist’s full availability for the chosen appointment type and current filter constraints, using getTherapistAvailabilityForAppointment. It displays an availability grid of returned slots and allows the user to select one slot as the candidate for booking.

Receives the currently selected appointment slot from the therapist-availability-panel and, when the user confirms, calls createDraftAppointmentFromSlot. Handles success by redirecting to appointment_confirm.html with the returned appointmentId parameter, and handles failure by surfacing the error while allowing the user to choose another slot.

Manages pagination of therapist search results by adjusting limit and offset (or an equivalent page indicator) in the URL and triggering re-calls to searchTherapistsForAppointment while preserving the current appointment type and filters.
Provides a link from the booking context to the full therapist directory (therapists.html), constructing the destination URL with the current specialty, format, and zip filters as URL parameters.

Keeps the in-memory state of appointment type, filters, sorting, and pagination synchronized with the URL query string, and listens for browser navigation events (back/forward) to restore state and re-trigger the appropriate interface calls.

layout.grid_system: Implicit responsive grid, likely a 12-column layout with a centered max-width content container (approx. 1080–1200px) and full-bleed hero image band.
layout.layout_pattern: Use a single, centered content column beneath the global header and a full-width hero band titled 'Book Appointment'. All interactive booking components are stacked vertically in a clear, linear flow: a subtle back/navigation link at the top of the content column, then appointment type selection, then filters, followed by the therapist results list with pagination, and finally the selected therapist’s availability panel and booking controls. Non-visual controller components live at the page root and do not alter the visual flow.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: Use a single, centered content column beneath the global header and a full-width hero band titled 'Book Appointment'. All interactive booking components are stacked vertically in a clear, linear flow: a subtle back/navigation link at the top of the content column, then appointment type selection, then filters, followed by the therapist results list with pagination, and finally the selected therapist’s availability panel and booking controls. Non-visual controller components live at the page root and do not alter the visual flow.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: On desktop, the main booking content sits in a centered column occupying roughly 60–70% of the viewport width, with a max width around 1080–1200px and generous left/right whitespace. The hero section above is full-bleed with the leaf imagery and the centered 'Book Appointment' title. Vertical gaps between major blocks (type selector, filters, results list, availability, booking CTA) are in the 32–48px range, echoing the spacious, calm rhythm of the mockup. Within each block, internal padding uses 16–24px increments to maintain comfort without feeling cluttered.
layout_strategies.content_density: Maintain low content density: relatively few controls per row, generous line-height, and open spacing between list items and form elements. Filters and selectors are not crammed together; each field has enough breathing room to be scannable and unintimidating. Therapist entries are moderately sized vertical cards with ample padding and comfortable spacing between them. The availability grid presents a limited set of slots in a loose grid with clear separation, avoiding tight, calendar-like density.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: The eye starts at the familiar header (logo left, nav right), then is drawn into the hero with the centered 'Book Appointment' title. From there, attention moves down to the top-left of the content column where the 'View therapists' link or similar breadcrumb sits. The user then naturally follows the strong vertical path: appointment type selector at the top, then the filter bar, then scanning down the therapist results list. At the bottom of the list, pagination provides a quiet decision point; beneath that, the availability panel for the selected therapist and a clear booking action area form the final focal point, guiding the user toward confirming an appointment.

layout.overall_description: Below the site’s consistent header and a full-width hero band with a soft leaf photograph and a centered 'Book Appointment' title, the page transitions into a light, slightly green-tinted background that holds the booking flow. In the middle of the viewport, a single, centered column—about two-thirds of the screen width—contains all booking controls in a calm, vertical sequence. At the very top of this column, a small, left-aligned link allows users to return to the general therapists view, giving a gentle, unobtrusive breadcrumb. A little lower, a softly framed appointment type selector spans most of the column, anchoring the start of the interaction. Directly underneath, a horizontal filter bar remains airy and uncluttered, with inputs aligned left but plenty of whitespace. The therapist results list follows, made of vertically stacked, full-width cards with enough padding and spacing to feel relaxed rather than busy. Below the list, minimalist pagination controls allow quiet navigation through pages. After a therapist is selected, an availability panel appears below the pagination, still within the same central column, showing a simple grid of time slots with roomy spacing. At the bottom of this panel, a short booking controller area summarizes the selection and surfaces a clear primary button to move forward, with generous margin before the footer so the ending of the flow feels deliberate and unhurried. Throughout, the design remains light, centered, and linear, echoing the original mockup’s calm, nature-inspired aesthetic.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: This component is non-visual and conceptually sits at the very top of the booking page’s structure, just beneath the global layout (header and hero) and wrapping the main content column. It initializes default appointment types, filters, and any preselected therapist before any visible elements render. It has no height, width, or visible styling and does not affect spacing; other components are laid out as if it were not present, simply benefitting from the initialized state.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Non-rendered controller at the page root; no visual footprint, no scroll behavior, and no impact on spacing or positioning.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The appointment-type selector is the first prominent interactive element in the main content column. It is placed directly below the bottom edge of the hero band, leaving a generous 40–48px vertical gap so it does not crowd the hero. If a 'View therapists' link is present, this selector sits about 16–24px beneath that link. The selector’s container is centered in the viewport but aligns its contents to the left, occupying roughly 70–80% of the column width on desktop, with 10–15% whitespace flanking it on each side. It appears as a softly framed horizontal block (for example, segmented buttons or a dropdown plus label) with 16–24px internal padding on all sides and 24–32px of vertical spacing below, clearly separating it from the filter bar that follows. On smaller screens, the selector simply shrinks with the column and any horizontal arrangement of options gracefully wraps into multiple rows while still maintaining the single-column feel.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Standard block-level element within the central column; scrolls normally with the page and maintains its relative position beneath the hero and any breadcrumb link.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The appointment filter bar sits immediately below the appointment-type selector, separated by approximately 24–32px of vertical whitespace to create a distinct but related section. It spans the full width of the content column (about 60–70% of the viewport width), with its inputs left-aligned within that space. The bar feels like a light horizontal strip: 16–20px of vertical padding and 16–24px of horizontal padding inside the container, so each filter control (such as date, modality, location) has ample room around it. On desktop, a small number of key filters are arranged in a single row with soft 16px gaps between fields; on narrower screens, these filters stack vertically, each taking the full width of the column while preserving comfortable spacing between them. There is a clear 32–40px gap below the filter bar before the therapist results list begins, reinforcing the low-density, step-by-step reading flow.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Standard block within the central column; scrolls with the page. Filter fields reflow responsively but remain confined to the single-column area.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The therapist results list begins about 32–40px below the filter bar, occupying the full width of the central content column. It presents a vertical stack of therapist entries, each rendered as a simple, full-width card on the light background. Every card has 16–24px of internal padding on all sides and is separated from the next card by a 16–24px vertical gap, giving each therapist ample breathing room. Text within each card (name, specialties, brief description) is left-aligned with generous line-height, and there are no tight multi-column arrangements inside the cards—information flows top-to-bottom. The list itself may span multiple screens in height; as the user scrolls down, the cards continue in a single column. At the bottom edge of the last visible card, there is a 24–32px vertical gap before the pagination controls appear, making the transition between the list and pagination distinct but gentle.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Standard vertically flowing list within the central column; scrolls normally. The number of cards shown per page is managed by pagination, but visually remains a simple stacked column of therapist entries.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The therapist availability panel is positioned below the results and pagination area, forming the next major block in the same central column. Visually, it begins about 32–40px under the pagination controls, making it feel like the second step after choosing a therapist from the list. The panel spans the full width of the content column and appears as a lightly differentiated section—either with a very subtle tinted background or a soft outline—to signal that it is contextually related but functionally distinct. Inside, a compact heading such as 'Availability for [Therapist Name]' is left-aligned at the top with 16–24px padding from the panel edges. Below the heading, a simple grid or list of time slots is arranged in a few rows: each slot is a small pill-like element with ample 8–12px gaps between slots horizontally and vertically, avoiding any dense calendar appearance. The overall height of the panel is moderate, focusing on a manageable set of upcoming times rather than an exhaustive calendar. There is a 24–32px vertical gap below this panel before the booking controller starts, visually grouping them as part of the same decision area.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Standard block-level section in the central column; scrolls with the page. It appears or updates when a therapist is selected but occupies the same general area in the layout, preventing sudden, jarring shifts higher up the page.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The slot booking controller is anchored directly beneath the therapist availability panel, separated by about 24–32px of vertical whitespace to distinguish it as the final action area. It remains within the boundaries of the central content column, with left-aligned content and enough horizontal padding (16–24px) to align visually with the elements above. The controller presents a brief, concise summary of the selected appointment (therapist, type, date, time) in one or two short lines, followed by a prominent primary action button (for example, 'Continue' or 'Confirm Appointment') aligned to the left, with the option to center it on smaller screens if necessary. The button has comfortable padding and stands out through color, but it is still harmonious with the calm palette. There is a generous 48–64px margin below this component before any footer content, allowing the end of the booking step to feel deliberate and spacious rather than cramped against the bottom of the page.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Standard block at the bottom of the booking flow within the central column; scrolls with the page. No sticky or floating behavior, ensuring a simple, linear progression.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The pagination controls sit directly below the therapist results list, with a 24–32px vertical gap from the last therapist card to provide a subtle separation. They occupy the same central column width as the list but are visually lighter and more compact. The controls are horizontally centered within the column, but the entire pagination strip stays within the left and right boundaries of the content container. Individual pagination elements (page numbers, next/previous) are spaced with 8–12px gaps so they are easy to tap or click without feeling crowded. The overall vertical height of the pagination area is modest, with about 16–20px of vertical padding above and below the controls. A further 32–40px gap below the pagination separates it from the therapist availability panel, ensuring the user perceives the list navigation as distinct from the time selection area.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Standard inline block inside the central column; scrolls normally. Elements are centered within the column but do not stick to any viewport edge.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The 'view therapists' link (or similar navigation link) appears as a subtle text link at the very top of the main booking content column, immediately below the hero band. There is a comfortable 32–40px vertical gap between the bottom of the hero and this link so it does not feel cramped against the large hero title. The link itself is left-aligned, occupying only as much width as its text requires, with ample whitespace to the right, reinforcing the calm, minimalist look. It might appear as a small, understated breadcrumb-style element (e.g., '← View all therapists'), with a 16–24px vertical gap beneath it before the appointment-type selector begins. This placement makes it easy to discover yet visually quiet, so it does not compete with the main booking controls below.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Standard inline text link anchored at the top of the central column; scrolls with the page. No sticky or floating behavior.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: This component is non-visual and operates behind the scenes across the entire booking page. Conceptually, it sits at the same level as the booking-page-init controller, attached to the overall page container, synchronizing the current booking state (selected type, filters, therapist, and time slot) with the URL. It does not occupy any space in the layout, has no width or height, and does not influence the positioning or spacing of visible components.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Non-rendered controller attached to the page-level context; no visual footprint, no scroll or positioning behavior.

name: Appointment Confirmation
title: Appointment Confirmation
description: Page to review draft appointment details, enter client contact information, and finalize the appointment booking.

core_features: Load draft appointment details using the appointmentId URL parameter via getAppointmentDraftDetails.
Display a read-only summary of the selected therapist, appointment type, date, time, and session format.
Collect required client contact details (first name, last name, phone, email) in a validation-aware form.
Submit client contact details and finalize the booking via confirmAppointment.
Show success or error feedback after confirmation, including the confirmed appointment details.
Provide navigation back to the home page after successful confirmation.
user_workflows:
Workflow 1: Open appointment confirmation and review details
1. User arrives on appointment_confirm.html with a query parameter appointmentId.
2. On page load, the system reads appointmentId from the URL.
3. The page calls getAppointmentDraftDetails(appointmentId).
4. While waiting for the response, a loading state is shown instead of the appointment summary and form.
5. On success, the page stores the returned appointment, therapist, appointmentType, and appointmentSlot in page state.
6. The appointment summary section displays the therapist name, appointment type, session format, and date/time from the loaded data.
7. If the returned appointment already has client contact fields populated (for a previously started draft), those values pre-fill the contact form fields.

Workflow 2: Enter contact details and confirm the appointment
1. After reviewing the summary, the user enters first name, last name, phone, and email in the contact form.
2. As the user types, the form updates its internal state and may perform basic client-side validation (e.g., required fields not empty, email format, phone non-empty).
3. The user clicks the primary submit button (e.g., "Confirm Appointment" / "Book Now").
4. The form validates all required fields; if validation fails, it prevents submission and highlights the fields with errors.
5. If validation passes, the form calls confirmAppointment with parameters: appointmentId (from URL), clientFirstName, clientLastName, clientPhone, and clientEmail (from form state).
6. While waiting for confirmAppointment to return, the submit button is disabled and a submission-in-progress state is shown.
7. If confirmAppointment.success is true, the page updates the appointment object in state with the returned appointment (now with status typically updated to confirmed) and shows a success banner or section using confirmAppointment.message and the appointment details.
8. If confirmAppointment.success is false, the page shows an error message using confirmAppointment.message, keeps the form data, and allows the user to correct data or retry submission.

Workflow 3: Handle missing/invalid appointmentId and post-confirmation navigation
1. If appointment_confirm.html is opened without an appointmentId in the URL, the page cannot call getAppointmentDraftDetails and instead shows an error/invalid-state message instructing the user to return to the booking flow.
2. If getAppointmentDraftDetails fails (e.g., invalid or expired appointmentId), the page shows an error state and does not show the confirmation form, offering a link to restart booking.
3. After a successful confirmation (confirmAppointment.success is true), a "Back to home" link becomes visible.
4. When the user clicks this link, the browser navigates to index.html (no additional parameters).
5. Optionally, the page may keep displaying the confirmed appointment summary so the user can review details before or instead of navigating away.
interactions: On page load: Read the appointmentId query parameter from the URL. If present, trigger getAppointmentDraftDetails(appointmentId). If absent, show an error state indicating that the appointment cannot be loaded.

On successful getAppointmentDraftDetails response: Store appointment, therapist, appointmentType, and appointmentSlot in shared page state. Switch from loading state to showing the summary and contact form components.
On failed getAppointmentDraftDetails response (e.g., network or data error): Show an error message area and hide or disable the confirmation form and submit button.
On text input change in the contact form fields (first name, last name, phone, email): Update the internal form state for each field and optionally clear previous validation errors for that field.
On field blur in the contact form: Run per-field validation (e.g., required, email pattern). If invalid, mark the field with an error state that is visible to the user.

On form submit (click on primary confirm button or pressing Enter): Prevent default form submission, run full-form validation (all required fields). If any errors exist, do not call confirmAppointment and display validation feedback.

On successful form validation: Call confirmAppointment with parameters from URL (appointmentId) and form state (clientFirstName, clientLastName, clientPhone, clientEmail). Set a submitting flag to disable the form and button.

On confirmAppointment response with success = true: Update appointment in state with the response appointment, clear the submitting flag, display a success banner or section using the response message, and keep the summary visible using the updated appointment data.
On confirmAppointment response with success = false: Clear the submitting flag, display an error message using response.message, and keep the form data so the user can adjust and retry.
On click of "Back to home" (shown only after a successful confirmation): Navigate the browser to index.html, ending the appointment confirmation flow.

On manual URL change (user editing appointmentId in the address bar and reloading): The page re-runs the load logic for the new appointmentId and re-fetches draft details with getAppointmentDraftDetails.
page_functionality.state_logic: The primary state driver is the appointmentId URL parameter on appointment_confirm.html. On initial load, the page parses appointmentId from the query string. If appointmentId is missing or empty, the page immediately enters an invalid-state mode (no API call) and displays appropriate messaging. If appointmentId is present, the page calls getAppointmentDraftDetails(appointmentId) and stores the returned appointment, therapist, appointmentType, and appointmentSlot in in-memory page state. This state is then bound to the summary and form components. The URL appointmentId is considered the source of truth; if the user modifies the URL and reloads, the page discards any previous in-memory state and fetches new data for the new appointmentId. No localStorage or persistent client storage is used. After confirmAppointment is called and returns success, the page updates its in-memory appointment state with the returned confirmed appointment. The URL does not need to change; the page uses an internal flag (e.g., isConfirmed) derived from either the appointment.status or confirmAppointment.success to switch from a pre-confirmation to a post-confirmation view (e.g., showing success messaging and the 'Back to home' link) while keeping the same appointmentId in the URL.

components.functionality: Reads appointmentId from the URL, calls getAppointmentDraftDetails to load draft appointment data, and exposes loading, success, and error states to other components.
event_handlers: onInit: Parse appointmentId from the URL query string; if present, call getAppointmentDraftDetails(appointmentId); if absent, set error state to "missing appointment".
onGetAppointmentDraftDetailsSuccess: Store appointment, therapist, appointmentType, and appointmentSlot in shared page state; set loading=false and error=null.
onGetAppointmentDraftDetailsError: Set error state with the error information; set loading=false; clear any existing appointment-related state.
onUrlChangeReload: If the page is reloaded with a different appointmentId, repeat the onInit logic to fetch the new draft.

components.functionality: Displays read-only information about the draft appointment for verification, including therapist identity, appointment type, session format, date/time, and duration, using data loaded by appointment-draft-loader.
event_handlers: onRender: If loading is true, show a loading indicator instead of the summary; if error exists, hide this component.
onDataChange: Whenever the appointment, therapist, appointmentType, or appointmentSlot in shared state changes, re-render to reflect the latest information.

onClickChangeAppointment: Optional; navigate back to the previous booking page (e.g., using browser history back or a link to book_appointment.html with appropriate parameters) without altering the current appointment data on this page.

components.functionality: Collects client_first_name, client_last_name, client_phone, and client_email required to confirm the appointment. Handles client-side validation and prepares data for confirmAppointment.
event_handlers: onInit: If the loaded appointment contains client_first_name, client_last_name, client_phone, or client_email (from a saved draft), pre-populate the corresponding form fields with those values.
onChangeFirstName: Update internal form state for clientFirstName and clear any first-name validation error.
onChangeLastName: Update internal form state for clientLastName and clear any last-name validation error.
onChangePhone: Update internal form state for clientPhone and clear any phone validation error.
onChangeEmail: Update internal form state for clientEmail and clear any email validation error.
onBlurField: For the blurred field, run validation (e.g., required or basic pattern check) and set or clear the corresponding validation error state.
onValidateForm: When called, check that all required fields are present and that email and phone meet basic format rules; return a validation result object used by onSubmit.

onSubmit: Prevent the default submit action, call onValidateForm, and if valid, trigger confirmAppointment with appointmentId from the URL and the form field values. If invalid, set validation errors so they are shown next to fields. While waiting for confirmAppointment, set a submitting flag and disable inputs and the submit button.
onDisableConditions: Disable the submit button if loading is true, error exists from the loader, submitting is true, or required fields are empty.

components.functionality: Bridges the contact-details-form submission with the confirmAppointment interface and manages the resulting success/error state across the page.
event_handlers: onConfirmAppointmentCall: Invoked by contact-details-form.onSubmit when validation passes; calls confirmAppointment with (appointmentId, clientFirstName, clientLastName, clientPhone, clientEmail).

onConfirmAppointmentSuccess: If response.success is true, update the appointment in shared state with the returned appointment, set an isConfirmed flag, clear submitting state, and propagate the success message to the confirmation-status-banner component.

onConfirmAppointmentFailure: If response.success is false or an error occurs, clear submitting state and propagate the error message to the confirmation-status-banner component without clearing the user's form input.
onResetAfterError: Optionally allow the user to adjust input and resubmit, reusing the same handler.

components.functionality: Shows feedback about the confirmation attempt, including success messages and confirmed appointment details, or error messages if confirmation fails.
event_handlers: onRender: If there is no confirmation attempt yet, render nothing or a neutral state. If confirmAppointment has been called, display either success or error message based on the last response.

onSuccessState: When confirmAppointment succeeds, display a success message (using response.message when available), and optionally show key fields from the confirmed appointment (e.g., date/time, therapist, and status).
onErrorState: When confirmAppointment fails, display an error message derived from response.message or error information and keep the contact form visible for retry.

onDismissMessage: Allow the user to hide/dismiss the banner while keeping the confirmed state or error state internally, so other components (like navigation) can still know whether the appointment is confirmed.

components.functionality: Controls top-level loading and error display based on the state of getAppointmentDraftDetails and the presence of the appointmentId URL parameter.
event_handlers: onInit: If appointmentId is missing in the URL, set a top-level invalid-state flag and show a message indicating that the appointment cannot be loaded.
onLoaderLoading: While appointment-draft-loader is in a loading state, show a loading indicator and prevent the contact form from being interactive.
onLoaderError: If appointment-draft-loader reports an error, show a descriptive error section and disable or hide the confirmation form and submit button.
onRetryClick: Optionally allow the user to retry calling getAppointmentDraftDetails for the same appointmentId if the error is transient (e.g., network issue).

components.functionality: Provides navigation options after the appointment is successfully confirmed, including a link back to the home page.
event_handlers: onRender: If appointment state indicates status is confirmed (or isConfirmed flag is true), render a link or button labeled to return to the home page; otherwise, hide this component.
onClickBackToHome: Navigate to index.html when the user activates the "Back to home" control.

onPreventPrematureNavigation: Before confirmation (when status is draft), do not display or enable the back-to-home shortcut to avoid users leaving unintentionally mid-process (unless the design intentionally allows it as a separate control).

layout.grid_system: Implicit responsive grid, likely a 12-column layout with a centered max-width content container (approx. 1080–1200px) and full-bleed hero image band.
layout.layout_pattern: The page keeps a single, linear, top-to-bottom flow. At the top is the existing site header (logo left, navigation right), followed by a full-width hero band with a soft, blurred nature photograph and a centered page title, e.g. “Appointment Confirmation.” Below the hero, the main content sits in a single centered column that uses approximately 60–70% of the viewport width on desktop, with generous side margins. Within this column the components are stacked in this order: loading/status at the very top (appointment-draft-loader and/or confirmation-status-banner / loading-and-error-state-handler), then the appointment-summary-panel, followed by the contact-details-form, then the confirm-appointment-integration (confirm button area), and finally post-confirmation-navigation at the bottom once the appointment is successfully confirmed.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: The page keeps a single, linear, top-to-bottom flow. At the top is the existing site header (logo left, navigation right), followed by a full-width hero band with a soft, blurred nature photograph and a centered page title, e.g. “Appointment Confirmation.” Below the hero, the main content sits in a single centered column that uses approximately 60–70% of the viewport width on desktop, with generous side margins. Within this column the components are stacked in this order: loading/status at the very top (appointment-draft-loader and/or confirmation-status-banner / loading-and-error-state-handler), then the appointment-summary-panel, followed by the contact-details-form, then the confirm-appointment-integration (confirm button area), and finally post-confirmation-navigation at the bottom once the appointment is successfully confirmed.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: The header is a modest horizontal band with generous vertical padding (16–24px) for an airy feel. The hero band is full-width and visually prominent, taking roughly 30–40% of the initial viewport height on desktop, with the title centered both horizontally and vertically within it. The main content background is a light green-tinted panel spanning edge-to-edge; inside it, a max-width container (about 1080–1200px) holds the central column. Vertical space between major sections (hero to content, summary to form, form to actions) follows a 32–48px rhythm, with internal padding of 16–24px around the summary panel, form, and banners. No component exceeds the width of the central content column, preserving a calm, contained layout.
layout_strategies.content_density: Content is kept low-density with clear separation between components. Text blocks are short and broken into headings, small paragraphs, and vertically spaced fields. Line height is comfortable (around 1.5–1.7), and bullet or field rows have 8–12px vertical spacing. The summary panel and status banners use concise text with plenty of breathing room around it; the form stacks fields vertically with clear spacing between groups (16–24px) to avoid visual clutter and cognitive overload.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: The visual flow mirrors the mockup: the eye starts at the logo on the top-left, moves across the top navigation, then drops down to the large, centered hero title. From the hero, the eye travels down into the main content, first encountering any status/loading information, then naturally continuing down to the appointment summary as the primary reference. From there, the user flows into the contact-details-form, ending on the confirm-appointment-integration (primary call-to-action) near the lower part of the column. After confirmation, the confirmation-status-banner appears near the top of the content column, reinforcing success, and the user’s gaze is then guided downward to the post-confirmation-navigation options. This creates a gentle Z-to-vertical pattern with a clear, low-stress reading path.

layout.overall_description: The page preserves the calm, minimalist structure of the original mockup. A slim, airy header sits at the top with the counseling practice’s logo aligned on the left and navigation links on the right. Directly beneath, a full-width hero band with a softly blurred leaf photograph spans the viewport, with the page title “Appointment Confirmation” centered in a classic serif typeface, lending a gentle, reassuring tone. Below the hero, the primary content rests on a light green-tinted background that stretches edge-to-edge, signaling a distinct, focused area for completing the booking. Within this background, a single central column (about two-thirds of the screen width on desktop, capped around 1080–1200px) holds all interactive components in a clean vertical stack. At the very top of this column, loading or status banners appear as needed, followed by a softly framed appointment summary card that reassures the user of the details they are about to confirm. Beneath that, the contact details form continues the vertical rhythm with well-spaced input fields, culminating in a clearly defined confirmation action area anchored near the bottom of the column. After successful confirmation, a gentle success banner appears near the top of this same column, and subtle navigation options to return home, visit the client portal, or schedule another appointment sit at the bottom. Generous vertical spacing between each block, combined with consistent margins and gentle color contrasts, creates a quiet, orderly flow down the page that feels safe and unhurried.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The appointment-draft-loader occupies the same vertical region as the main content column immediately below the hero section. When active, it temporarily replaces the appointment-summary-panel and contact-details-form. It is centered both horizontally and vertically within the central content column, which sits on the light green-tinted background and spans about 60–70% of the viewport width. The loader’s visual elements (spinner and brief loading text) appear in the middle of an empty, softly padded box, with 32–48px of top margin from the bottom edge of the hero and 24–32px of internal padding. There is ample whitespace above and below the loader, so the user perceives a calm waiting state rather than a crowded or urgent one.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: By default, the loader is displayed inline in the normal document flow at the top of the main content area, pushing other components below it out of view. Once loading completes, this component collapses and yields its vertical space to the appointment-summary-panel and subsequent components without leaving gaps.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The appointment-summary-panel is the first substantive content block within the main column, appearing directly beneath any active status or error banners and roughly 32–48px below the hero image’s lower edge. It appears as a full-width card within the central column, matching the 60–70% viewport width constraint and centered on the page. The panel has a subtle, soft border or shadow and 16–24px internal padding on all sides, with its content (appointment date, time, therapist, location, session type) stacked vertically in short lines. The heading (e.g., “Review your appointment details”) sits at the top left of the panel, with details following beneath, maintaining a left-aligned, readable column. Below the last detail line, there is a 16–24px bottom padding before the card ends. The panel has 32px of vertical space separating it from the component above (or from the hero if no banner is present) and 32–40px of spacing beneath it before the contact-details-form begins, ensuring it feels like a distinct, calmly emphasized reference area.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: The panel is a standard block-level element within the central column, expanding vertically with content but fixed in place as content scrolls. It does not stick or float; instead, it anchors the user’s reading flow at the top of the main content area, ahead of the form.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The contact-details-form sits directly below the appointment-summary-panel, separated by a comfortable vertical gap of about 32–40px. It spans the full width of the central content column (again about 60–70% of the viewport, constrained to roughly 1080–1200px), aligned left with the summary panel. The form appears as a light, minimally bordered or borderless section with 16–24px internal padding. Its heading (e.g., “Your contact details”) is left-aligned at the top, followed by a vertically stacked layout of fields (name, email, phone, consent checkboxes, notes) with 8–16px spacing between individual fields and 16–24px between logical groups. Labels sit above fields, and any supporting text uses smaller, softly colored typography to maintain low visual weight. The bottom of the form section maintains about 24px of padding before transitioning into the confirm-appointment-integration area below, so the button section feels visually connected but not cramped.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: The form flows naturally as a full-width block within the central column and grows vertically as fields are added. It is scrollable only as part of the page; no internal scrolling is used. It remains in normal flow with no fixed positioning, supporting a calm, predictable reading pattern.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The confirm-appointment-integration area is directly attached to the bottom of the contact-details-form, appearing as a horizontal action bar within the same central column. It begins about 16–24px below the last form field or consent checkbox, maintaining the vertical rhythm. The primary “Confirm Appointment” button sits left-aligned with the form fields, occupying roughly the width of its label plus 24–32px horizontal padding on each side and 8–12px vertical padding, consistent with the established button sizing. If any secondary actions (e.g., “Go back” or “Edit details”) are present, they appear to the left or right with lighter styling and at least 16px of horizontal spacing between them. There is ample whitespace (32–40px) beneath this action area before the next component (post-confirmation-navigation, or just the end of the content if the appointment has not yet been confirmed), preventing the button from feeling crowded against the page bottom. On narrow viewports, the button area remains left-aligned but may visually feel centered within the smaller column, keeping the vertical, single-column emphasis intact.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: This component is inline and part of the standard document flow, positioned at the end of the form. It does not use sticky or floating behavior; instead, the user naturally scrolls down to reach it after completing the form, reinforcing a deliberate, unhurried confirmation step.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The confirmation-status-banner appears within the central content column at the very top of the main content stack, just below the hero section. When visible (typically after the user confirms the appointment), it stretches across the full width of the central column, with 16–24px of internal padding and rounded corners, set against a gently tinted background (e.g., soft green for success) that stands out from, but still harmonizes with, the page’s light green-tinted main background. The banner’s text is left-aligned, with a concise heading (e.g., “Your appointment is confirmed”) and a one- or two-line explanation beneath, separated by 4–8px of vertical space. There is a 24–32px margin below the banner before the appointment-summary-panel, so the banner feels clearly distinguished yet closely associated with the rest of the content. If no confirmation has occurred yet, the banner area remains absent, allowing the summary panel to move up and occupy that space.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: The banner behaves as an inline block in the content flow. It appears and disappears depending on state, pushing the rest of the content downward when active. It is not sticky; as the user scrolls, it moves offscreen like other content, preventing any feeling of persistent pressure.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The loading-and-error-state-handler manifests as contextual messages aligned with the components they relate to, primarily in the upper portion of the main content column. Global errors (e.g., failing to load the appointment draft or confirm the booking) appear as a full-width banner placed in the same slot as the confirmation-status-banner—just below the hero and above the appointment-summary-panel—with a softly tinted background (muted red or amber) and 16–24px padding. Local, field-level errors appear directly beneath the relevant form field labels in the contact-details-form, using concise text and a calm, slightly warmer color, with 4–8px vertical spacing so they are visible but not overwhelming. The overall approach keeps each error on its own line with breathing room, ensuring the layout remains open even when multiple messages are present.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: This handler injects error or loading states into the normal layout flow rather than overlaying or blocking the interface. Global banners occupy the same structural position as the confirmation-status-banner, pushing other content down. Field-level messages expand the vertical height of their respective form rows without causing overlapping or fixed-position elements. When states resolve, these messages collapse, returning space to the surrounding components.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The post-confirmation-navigation area appears near the bottom of the main content column, only after the appointment has been successfully confirmed. It is positioned below the confirm-appointment-integration and/or after the confirmation-status-banner and summary panel, with about 40–48px of vertical space separating it from the last major section. This component consists of a small set of navigation options—such as buttons or text links like “Return to Home,” “View Client Portal,” or “Schedule another appointment”—arranged horizontally when space allows, with 16–24px horizontal spacing between items. The group is center-aligned within the central column, reinforcing a sense of closure and giving the user gentle next steps. There is generous whitespace (32–64px) below this navigation before the end of the tinted content area or the page footer, maintaining the airy, calm bottom edge of the page.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: This component is a standard inline block at the bottom of the main content flow. It does not stick or float; instead, it appears as a natural concluding element reached after scrolling through the confirmation details. If the confirmation has not occurred, this section remains hidden, and the layout simply ends after the confirm-appointment-integration.

name: Therapist Comparison
title: Therapist Comparison
description: Side-by-side comparison page that shows key information for therapists currently in the comparison list and lets users choose one therapist to move forward with or return to the directory.

core_features: Load the current comparison list (up to the maximum allowed therapists) using getTherapistComparisonState when the page initializes.

Synchronize the comparison list with URL parameters (e.g., therapist_compare.html?therapistIds={id1},{id2}) by reconciling URL therapistIds with the state returned by getTherapistComparisonState and updating via toggleTherapistInComparison.
Retrieve detailed comparison data (fees, sliding scale, formats, rating, availability, location) for each therapist in the comparison list using getTherapistComparisonDetails.

Render a side-by-side comparison grid that aligns attributes for each therapist: specialties, feesSummary, formatsAvailable, rating, hasWeekendAvailability, locationSummary, offers_free_consult, accepts_insurance and other available Therapist fields.
Allow users to remove individual therapists from the comparison list via toggleTherapistInComparison and immediately update both the UI and the therapistIds URL parameter.
Handle empty or incomplete comparison states (0 or 1 therapist) by showing an appropriate message and a clear path to return to the directory (therapists.html).

Provide per-therapist quick actions: navigate to therapist_profile.html with the selected therapistId, and offer a generic "Start booking" CTA that navigates to book_appointment.html with appointmentTypeCode set (for example) to individual_counseling.

Respect comparison constraints exposed by the interfaces (maxSelectable and canCompare) to conditionally enable or disable comparison-specific UI (e.g., hiding certain side-by-side cues when canCompare is false).
user_workflows:
Workflow 1 – Open comparison page from directory:
1) User selects therapists to compare on therapists.html and clicks the directory's Compare action which navigates to therapist_compare.html with a therapistIds URL parameter (e.g., therapist_compare.html?therapistIds={id1},{id2}).
2) On page load, the comparison state controller component calls getTherapistComparisonState to obtain the current therapistIds, therapists, maxSelectable, and canCompare.
3) If the URL therapistIds parameter is present and differs from the returned therapistIds, the controller reconciles the two: it may call toggleTherapistInComparison for any therapistId in the URL that is not yet in the state (up to maxSelectable) and remove any extra therapists in state that are not in the URL, or alternatively update the URL to match a non-empty existing server-side state.
4) After state and URL are aligned, the controller calls getTherapistComparisonDetails to obtain detailed comparison data for the therapists in the list.
5) The comparison grid component receives this data and renders columns for each therapist with all available attributes and action buttons.
6) User reviews the comparison and decides which therapist to explore further or whether to return to the directory.

Workflow 2 – Remove a therapist from comparison:
1) User clicks a "Remove from comparison" control in the column for a given therapist.
2) The comparison grid emits an event with the therapistId to the controller component.
3) The controller calls toggleTherapistInComparison with that therapistId.
4) On successful response, the controller updates its internal state with the new therapistIds and therapists returned by toggleTherapistInComparison and also updates the therapistIds URL parameter to reflect the new list.
5) The controller re-calls getTherapistComparisonDetails (if at least one therapist remains) to refresh the comparison data.
6) If no therapists remain, the empty comparison state component is shown, and the side-by-side grid and comparison-specific cues are hidden.

Workflow 3 – View a therapist’s full profile from comparison:
1) User decides that one therapist looks promising and clicks a "View profile" button in that therapist’s comparison column.
2) The event handler reads the therapistId from the associated therapist object.
3) The browser navigates to therapist_profile.html with a therapistId query parameter (e.g., therapist_profile.html?therapistId={id}).
4) The comparison page does not alter the comparison list on this navigation; it relies on the destination page to handle its own state.

Workflow 4 – Start booking from comparison:
1) User decides to book a session and clicks a generic "Start booking" button shown in the comparison footer or near a therapist column.
2) If the action is generic (not tied to a specific therapist), the navigation goes to book_appointment.html with appointmentTypeCode set to an appropriate default such as individual_counseling (e.g., book_appointment.html?appointmentTypeCode=individual_counseling).
3) If the design includes a therapist-specific booking action, it can first navigate to therapist_profile.html?therapistId={id} where the user can choose an appointment type, or directly to book_appointment.html with appointmentTypeCode and context handled on that page.
4) The comparison list is left unchanged; users can return to the comparison via browser navigation as needed.

Workflow 5 – Handle invalid or over-limit URL therapistIds:
1) User or external link opens therapist_compare.html with therapistIds that exceed maxSelectable or contain therapistIds not recognized by getTherapistComparisonState/toggleTherapistInComparison.
2) The controller calls getTherapistComparisonState, then attempts to add therapistIds from the URL via toggleTherapistInComparison in order, stopping once maxSelectable is reached or if toggleTherapistInComparison fails for a given id.
3) Any invalid ids are ignored in the UI, and the controller updates the URL therapistIds parameter to reflect only the successfully tracked therapistIds.
4) If the resulting list is empty or cannot satisfy canCompare, the empty or limited comparison state is shown with guidance to return to the directory.
interactions: Click on "Back to directory" / "Return to search" link in the header or empty state navigates the browser to therapists.html, optionally preserving non-conflicting query parameters such as a "from=compare" flag.

On initial page load or when the URL therapistIds parameter changes (e.g., via browser back/forward), the controller component triggers getTherapistComparisonState followed, if needed, by toggleTherapistInComparison calls to reconcile state and then getTherapistComparisonDetails to load comparison data.
