Displays a list of crisis hotlines retrieved via getCrisisHotlines using hotlineType and isLocal URL parameters. Allows the user to select a hotline to add it as a crisis contact in the safety plan form.

Manages the editable list of crisis contacts in the safety plan. Supports adding, editing, and removing contacts, and listens for selections from the hotline-suggestions-panel to create new contacts pre-filled from CrisisHotline data.

Allows the user to create and edit a list of warning signs within the safety plan. It uses warningSignsExamples from getSafetyPlan as optional suggestions, which the user can insert into the list or use as guidance.
Allows the user to create and edit a list of coping strategies within the safety plan. It uses copingStrategiesExamples from getSafetyPlan as optional suggestions to help the user populate the list.

Wraps the crisis contacts, warning signs, and coping strategies editors into a single safety plan form. Handles form-level validation and submission, and prepares the payload for saveSafetyPlan based on the current draft SafetyPlan state.

Receives save requests from the safety-plan-form, calls saveSafetyPlan with the current crisisContacts, warningSigns, and copingStrategies, and processes the response. On success, it updates the in-memory SafetyPlan data, switches the page to view mode, and updates URL parameters including saveStatus. On error, it sets saveStatus to indicate failure and exposes the error message.

Provides a read-only view of the current safety plan, displaying crisis contacts, warning signs, and coping strategies from the SafetyPlan entity. It is shown when the page is in view mode and supports edit, print, and back-to-crisis-support actions.

Displays success or error messages based on the saveStatus URL parameter and the latest response from saveSafetyPlan. Allows the user to dismiss messages, after which it can trigger URL cleanup to remove saveStatus.

Manages in-page navigation between the crisis contacts, warning signs, and coping strategies sections. Reads and updates the section URL parameter and scrolls or focuses the corresponding component accordingly.
Provides a link or button that navigates the user back to crisis_support.html, appending URL parameters (such as a from indicator) to preserve context about where the user came from.

layout.grid_system: Implicit responsive grid, likely a 12-column layout with a centered max-width content container (approx. 1080–1200px) and full-bleed hero image band.
layout.layout_pattern: Use a calm, single-column, top-to-bottom flow centered in the viewport. Keep the existing global header (logo left, nav right) and a full-width hero band with a soft, nature-inspired image and the centered page title 'Safety Plan'. Below the hero, all page-specific components sit inside a max-width central column (approx. 1080–1200px, ~60–70% of viewport on desktop). The order within this column: back navigation link → notification banner → hotline suggestions panel → section anchor navigation → main safety plan form (with its subsections in sequence) → save action row → read-only view/print panel.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: Use a calm, single-column, top-to-bottom flow centered in the viewport. Keep the existing global header (logo left, nav right) and a full-width hero band with a soft, nature-inspired image and the centered page title 'Safety Plan'. Below the hero, all page-specific components sit inside a max-width central column (approx. 1080–1200px, ~60–70% of viewport on desktop). The order within this column: back navigation link → notification banner → hotline suggestions panel → section anchor navigation → main safety plan form (with its subsections in sequence) → save action row → read-only view/print panel.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: Reserve a modest vertical band for the header and a visually prominent full-width hero (roughly 30–40% of initial viewport height). Start the main content column with a generous top margin (48–64px) below the hero. Maintain wide side gutters so the central column feels airy, with text and panels spanning roughly 65% of page width on large screens and full width with padding on mobile. Within the column, panels get 16–24px internal padding and 24–40px vertical separation to maintain a spacious, calm rhythm.
layout_strategies.content_density: Keep density low: short headings, concise helper text, and vertically stacked fields. Provide generous line height for all labels and bullet lists (about 1.5x font size) and use 16–24px vertical spacing between grouped elements inside sections. Avoid multi-column field layouts; even when multiple inputs are related, they should stack vertically or in very simple small clusters to preserve clarity and reduce cognitive load in this sensitive, mental-health context.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: Maintain the mockup’s gentle Z-then-vertical flow. The eye travels from the global logo/navigation bar across the top, then down to the centered 'Safety Plan' hero title, then into the main column. Within the column, the eye first catches the subtle 'Back to Crisis Support' link at top left, then the notification banner if present, then the slightly emphasized hotline panel as an early safety resource. From there, the section anchor navigation provides a quick scan path for key plan sections, and the user moves down the main form in a straightforward vertical sequence: crisis contacts → warning signs → coping strategies. Finally, attention flows down to the save actions and then to the read-only safety plan view/print panel.

layout.overall_description: Below the site’s standard header and a full-width hero band with a soft leaf image and the centered heading 'Safety Plan', the page content begins in a single, centered column on a light green-tinted background. This column is comfortably narrow relative to the viewport, creating generous white space to the left and right. At the top of the column, a simple text link offers a way back to broader crisis support resources, followed by any status notifications. A calm, lightly bordered hotline panel sits next, giving immediate access to crisis numbers without feeling alarming. Just beneath, a slim horizontal section anchor strip lets visitors jump to crisis contacts, warning signs, coping strategies, or the plan preview, but it remains understated so the main vertical flow stays dominant. The core of the page is a tall but softly spaced form: three stacked sections guide the user through entering crisis contacts, recognizing warning signs, and listing coping strategies, each with clear headings and ample breathing room. At the bottom of the form, a modest action row anchors the primary save interaction. Underneath, a gentle, card-like preview panel displays the completed safety plan in a print-friendly, read-only layout. Everything is vertically aligned and paced with generous spacing, producing a calm, reassuring experience consistent with the original mockup’s minimalist, nature-inspired aesthetic.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: This non-visual data-loader sits logically at the top of the Safety Plan page’s component hierarchy, conceptually just below the hero section and above all interactive content. When active (e.g., during initial load), it can temporarily surface as a very light, semi-transparent veil centered over the main content column with a small, unobtrusive loading indicator in the middle. The veil should span the full width of the central content column (not the entire viewport) and extend from just below the hero’s bottom edge down to cover the form and preview areas, preserving the calm, focused central layout while indicating that content is being prepared.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Non-visual under normal circumstances. When loading, overlays the central content column with a centered spinner or progress indicator; positioned relative to the main content container, not fixed to the entire viewport.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The notification banner appears at the top of the main content column, directly below the generous top margin under the hero and just beneath (or, when not present, above where the back link sits). It spans the full width of the central column (roughly 65% of the viewport on desktop), with 16–20px padding on all sides and rounded corners, following the site’s soft visual language. There is 8–12px of space above and 16–24px below it. The banner remains a low-profile strip: text is left-aligned, with a calm accent color (soft green or soft yellow) used in the background or left border. It does not extend to full-page width, keeping it visually integrated with the content column and avoiding any sense of alarm.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static element in the vertical flow; appears and disappears without shifting the overall layout structure beyond the normal vertical reflow.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The 'Back to Crisis Support' link is the first interactive element in the main content column, positioned at the top left of the column just below the hero’s lower edge and above the notification banner (or directly above the hotline panel if no banner is present). It is left-aligned with the text baseline of the other content in the column, with about 8–12px of space below it. The link is styled subtly, in a muted green or gray, perhaps with a small left-pointing chevron icon, and does not dominate the page; it acts as a gentle breadcrumb-like escape route that aligns with the calm, minimalist design. It occupies only a single line of text and does not affect the column width.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static inline element anchored to the top of the main content flow; scrolls away with the page content.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The hotline suggestions panel sits near the top of the main content column, below the back navigation link and the notification banner. It is presented as a full-width panel within the central column, with 16–24px internal padding and a softly tinted background (slightly more saturated than the page background) and rounded corners. The panel’s content is arranged in a simple vertical stack: a short heading such as 'If you’re in immediate crisis' at the top, followed by a vertical list of 2–4 hotline suggestions with clear labels and phone numbers. Each line has generous line height (about 1.5x) and 8–12px vertical spacing. There is 24–32px of space below the panel before the section anchor navigation, maintaining the open, breathable rhythm of the layout.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static block element in the vertical flow. It expands vertically based on content but always matches the width of the central content column. No sticky behavior; simply scrolls with the page.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The section anchor navigation appears just below the hotline suggestions panel, still within the central content column. It is a slim horizontal strip, left-aligned, occupying the full column width but only a small vertical height (roughly 48–64px including padding). Inside, 3–4 anchor items ('Crisis Contacts', 'Warning Signs', 'Coping Strategies', 'Plan Preview') are arranged in a row, aligned to the left with even 16–24px gaps between them. Each anchor is styled as understated text or pill-shaped buttons with soft borders and minimal fill, keeping them visually quiet. There is ~16px padding above and below the row, and 24–32px of vertical space below the strip before the main form begins.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Initially positioned in the normal vertical flow under the hotline panel; on larger screens it can become gently sticky when the user scrolls past it, pinning to the top edge of the central content area (below the global header) to provide quick jumps between sections without feeling heavy or intrusive.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The safety plan form is the primary content block of the page and begins directly below the section anchor navigation. It occupies the full width of the central column and is conceptually framed as one tall, unified form section, though visually broken into clearly separated subsections. The form has a 24px top padding and 24–32px bottom padding, with its internal sections (crisis contacts, warning signs, coping strategies) stacked in a single column. Each subsection is separated by 32–40px vertical spacing to prevent crowding. The form fields themselves are left-aligned, with labels above inputs, and typical padding of 8–12px vertical and 16–20px horizontal inside input boxes. The overall form area extends down the page, maintaining a consistent left alignment and never splitting into multiple columns, emphasizing a calm, step-by-step progression.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static block element that dictates most of the page height. It grows vertically as the user adds items (e.g., additional contacts or strategies), pushing subsequent components (save row, view panel) downward in the natural vertical flow.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: Within the safety plan form, the crisis contact list editor is the first and most prominent subsection at the top. It sits flush with the form’s left edge and spans the full width of the form column. The section starts with a bold but not oversized heading (e.g., 'Crisis Contacts') and a short, single-paragraph helper text beneath, separated by about 8–12px. Below this, the list of contact input rows is arranged vertically: each contact row (name, relationship, phone, etc.) is stacked with 8–12px spacing between fields, and 16–24px spacing between different contacts. An 'Add another contact' control appears left-aligned under the last row with a gentle accent color. The entire section has about 24–32px of vertical space below it before the next subsection begins.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static subsection inside the main form; expands vertically as more contacts are added but remains full-width within the central column. No sticky or floating elements.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The warning signs editor is the second subsection inside the safety plan form, positioned directly below the crisis contact list editor with 32–40px of vertical separation. It shares the same full-width alignment and typographic hierarchy: a clear heading ('Warning Signs') left-aligned at the top, followed by brief explanatory text. Below this, the user’s warning signs are entered as a simple vertical list—either multiple text inputs or pill-like list items arranged in a single column. Each item has 8–12px spacing between them and a comfortable line height, maintaining the low-density feel. An 'Add another warning sign' control appears at the bottom, left-aligned. The section is visually self-contained, with about 24–32px of space before the coping strategies section begins.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static subsection within the form; grows in height as the list increases. It maintains the same width and alignment as other subsections, contributing to the overall single-column rhythm.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The coping strategies editor is the third subsection inside the safety plan form, placed below the warning signs editor with 32–40px vertical spacing. Like the others, it spans the full width of the central column and begins with a left-aligned heading ('Coping Strategies') and brief helper copy. Below, coping strategies are represented as a vertical list of inputs or text areas, each separated by 12–16px to allow for slightly longer descriptions while keeping the section feeling light. An 'Add another strategy' control sits below the last item. There is a generous 32–40px gap between the bottom of this section and the form’s action row (where the save handler connects), clearly demarcating content entry from actions.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static subsection inside the form, with height expanding based on the number and length of strategies. It remains full-width and aligned with the other subsections to maintain a consistent vertical flow.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The save handler is manifested visually as the form’s action area at the bottom of the safety plan form, directly following the coping strategies editor. This action row sits left-aligned with the form content and spans the width of the central column, but its key controls (e.g., a primary 'Save Safety Plan' button and possibly a secondary 'Save & Print' or 'Download PDF' link) are clustered on the left side. There is 24–32px of spacing above the button row and about 32px below before the view panel begins. Buttons use the site’s primary accent color in a soft, rounded style with 10–12px vertical and 20–24px horizontal padding, appearing substantial but not heavy. The area remains visually compact to avoid overwhelming the user.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Non-sticky, static action row anchored at the bottom of the form. Button enable/disable states and feedback are managed by the handler but do not alter its spatial position; the row simply moves up or down as the form’s height changes.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The safety plan view panel sits below the form and its action row, separated by a generous 40–48px vertical gap so it feels like a distinct but related phase: review and print. It spans the full width of the central content column, styled like a calm, read-only card with a slightly lighter or off-white background than the form, subtle border, and 24–32px internal padding. The panel begins with a small heading ('Your Safety Plan') at the top left, possibly paired with a discrete print/download control aligned to the right within the panel’s header row. Below, the plan content is laid out as simple, clearly labeled sections (Crisis Contacts, Warning Signs, Coping Strategies) stacked vertically, mirroring the form’s structure but in read-only text form with generous line height and 16–24px spacing between subsections. This panel may extend quite tall but keeps the same centered, single-column alignment to maintain cohesion.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static block below the form that scrolls with the page. On print, this panel can be treated as the primary printable area, but in normal view it simply occupies vertical space as a read-only reflection of the saved data.
